metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether eval_solver.run_case() already supports code-review overlay prompt IDs (it currently raises ValueError for them at eval_solver.py:171), and if not, either scope the harness extension into this story or reframe AC-2 as 'eval spec authored and validated offline (structure only)' rather than asserting a runnable passing result.
findings.finding: The plan's AC-2 ('Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval') asserts a runnable eval result, but eval_solver.run_case() has no execution arm for 'code-review-db-migrations' — it raises ValueError for any non-criterion, non-{completion-verifier,ticket-quality,spec-alignment} prompt ID (eval_solver.py:171). Without new solver work, the eval spec is an inert YAML artifact that cannot produce the asserted 'passing' result. This is a premature/cargo-cult AC: copying the eval-spec pattern from plan-review overlays without confirming the harness supports code-review prompt IDs.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC-2 / Files: 'eval spec + fixtures for the db-migrations overlay'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge that no `code-review-db-migrations.eval.yaml` or code-review overlay eval-solver arm currently exists, and add to the plan the work needed to either extend `eval_solver.run_case()` for code-review overlays or specify an alternative execution mechanism before AC2 can be satisfied.
findings.finding: AC2 asserts 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval,' implying a working eval harness for `code-review-db-migrations`. No `code-review-db-migrations.eval.yaml` (or any `code-review-*.eval.yaml`) exists in `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/`, and `eval_solver.run_case()` has no execution arm for code-review overlay prompt IDs — it raises `ValueError` for any prompt_id that is not `completion-verifier`, `ticket-quality`, `spec-alignment`, or a registered plan-review criterion.
findings.location: AC2: 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval; does not over-suppress valid patterns.' / Files: 'eval spec + fixtures for the db-migrations overlay (recall + no-fire cases)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py` (or the alternative eval execution mechanism) as a required edit target, since `eval_solver.run_case()` currently raises `ValueError` for any `code-review-*` prompt id.
findings.finding: `eval_solver.run_case()` has no execution arm for `code-review-db-migrations` (or any `code-review-*` overlay prompt id); its only non-criterion arms are `completion-verifier`, `ticket-quality`, and `spec-alignment`. Running AC-2's recall+no-fire eval against this overlay requires an unenumerated change to `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py` (or equivalent harness extension), which the plan does not list.
findings.location: AC-2 — 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval'; src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm or add the eval harness arm that can execute `code-review-db-migrations` as a prompt (eval_solver.run_case currently raises ValueError for this id) and specify the concrete eval file path, so AC2's 'passing recall + no-fire eval' claim is mechanically executable.
findings.finding: AC2 asserts 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval' for the db-migrations overlay, but eval_solver.run_case() (lines 106–171) has no execution arm for code-review overlay prompt IDs — it handles only completion-verifier, ticket-quality, spec-alignment, and plan-review criterion ids, raising ValueError for anything else. No code-review-db-migrations.eval.yaml exists in eval_specs/. The plan treats a working code-review overlay eval harness as a pre-existing capability when it does not exist.
findings.location: AC2: 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval'
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) add a dispatch arm to eval_solver.run_case for code-review overlay prompt IDs (routing through the existing agentic op scaffolding), or (b) document that the eval runs via a different mechanism (e.g., `rebar prompt eval` CLI against a new code-review-db-migrations.eval.yaml) and verify that path exists — then name the concrete file path in the plan's Files section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge and plan for the missing eval harness capability: `eval_solver.run_case()` has no dispatch arm for code-review overlay prompt IDs and no `code-review-db-migrations.eval.yaml` exists; either extend the solver to support code-review overlays or scope AC2 to a mechanism that already exists.
findings.finding: AC2 asserts 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval' for the db-migrations overlay, but eval_solver.run_case() has no execution arm for code-review overlay prompt IDs — it raises ValueError for any unrecognised non-criterion prompt_id (line 171) — and no code-review-db-migrations.eval.yaml exists in eval_specs/ (nor does any code-review-*.eval.yaml precedent). The eval capability AC2 depends on is absent.
findings.location: AC2 / Files: 'eval spec + fixtures for the db-migrations overlay'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a deliverable: 'extend eval_solver.run_case() with a code-review overlay arm (or .rebar/evals/code-review-db-migrations.eval.yaml + a compatible solver path) so the eval spec can actually execute.' Alternatively, restate AC2 as a manual spot-eval until a general code-review overlay eval harness lands in a separate story.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define how the db-migrations overlay eval will be executed — either add a dispatch arm to eval_solver.run_case() for code-review overlays (injecting a diff fixture) or specify an alternative eval mechanism — and document this in the plan.
findings.finding: eval_solver.run_case() has no dispatch arm for code-review-* prompts; the db-migrations overlay is an agentic diff-reader (not a plan-review criterion or one of the three hardcoded agentic reviewer ids), so AC2's 'passing recall + no-fire eval' has no runnable execution path in the existing eval infrastructure.
findings.location: AC2: 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval'; src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete file path (e.g., `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-db-migrations.eval.yaml`) to the Files section and define its dataset structure with recall + no-fire cases for each named pattern.
findings.finding: The eval spec file for the db-migrations overlay (the second Files entry: 'eval spec + fixtures for the db-migrations overlay') has no concrete file path specified, and no such file exists anywhere in src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/ or .rebar/evals/ — the artifact required by AC2 ('passing recall + no-fire eval') is entirely absent and unresolvable.
findings.location: Files section: 'eval spec + fixtures for the db-migrations overlay (recall + no-fire cases)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-db-migrations.eval.yaml` to the Files list and specify its required sections: `prompt: rebar-code-review-db-migrations`, `scorers` (`recall_on_seeded_defects`, `no_fire_on_good_cases`), and a `dataset` with one recall case and one no-fire case per named pattern.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the FP-discrimination guard conditions for the db-migrations overlay (e.g., 'idempotent re-run of an already-applied migration', 'test-only migration with no schema effect', 'additive nullable column — safe without backfill') so an executor can write and test them.
findings.finding: The FP-guards ('both halves') are cited as a required deliverable in AC1 and the What section but are never enumerated anywhere — their trigger conditions, discrimination rules, and value vocabulary are entirely absent, leaving an executor with no specification to implement.
findings.location: What section: 'FP-guards (both halves)'; AC1: 'db-migrations overlay carries both recall patterns and FP-discrimination guards'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and cite the specific source document (file path or external reference) for the db-migrations domain content ('dso content') that provides the recall patterns and FP-guards; if no repo-visible document exists, explicitly scope authoring the domain content as part of this story rather than implying it is a lookup.
findings.finding: The plan says to enrich the overlay 'with dso content' for both recall patterns and FP-guards, but 'dso' has zero matches in src/rebar/llm/ and the only dso artifact in the repo (docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/criteria/dso-criteria-specs.md) covers plan-review criteria (F1, E1, E2, etc.), not code-review overlay content for the db-migrations dimension. The executor has no named, repo-visible reference document for the specific db-migrations domain patterns the plan intends to pull from. This risks either cargo-cult reuse (copying content that doesn't apply) or NIH authoring (inventing domain content without a cited reference).
findings.location: What section — 'Enrich the db-migrations overlay prompt with dso content'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the opaque 'dso content' reference with a concrete, locatable artifact path or ticket reference that an executor can read to obtain the db-migrations recall patterns and FP-guards.
findings.finding: The plan's enrichment is predicated on 'dso content' as the named source of both the recall patterns and FP-guards, but no artifact, document, or file named or aliased 'dso' exists anywhere under `src/rebar/llm/` — the codebase shows 'dso' only as an unrelated tool/project name (reconciler tests, experiment run data). An executor has no verifiable source to draw the db-migrations recall patterns and FP-guards from.
findings.location: What section: 'Enrich the db-migrations overlay prompt with dso content: recall patterns AND FP-guards (both halves)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'does not over-suppress valid patterns' with a concrete, measurable criterion — e.g. name a set of positive-recall fixtures that must continue to fire after FP-guard additions, or fold this into the fixture count in AC2.
findings.finding: 'Does not over-suppress valid patterns' in AC2 is subjective and has no defined threshold or proving command, making it unmeasurable in-session.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: '…does not over-suppress valid patterns.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand AC1 (or add a new AC) to enumerate the specific recall patterns that must be present in the overlay, matching the five patterns named in the What section, so every described deliverable has a covering, verifiable criterion.
findings.finding: The five specific recall patterns named in 'What' (delete+replace migration symmetry, destructive migrations, backfill correctness, expand/contract sequencing, lock duration) have no individual or enumerated coverage in any AC — AC1 only asserts 'recall patterns' exist collectively, so an implementation that omits one or more named patterns still satisfies every AC as written.
findings.location: What section (named patterns) vs. Acceptance Criteria AC1
findings.suggested_fix: AC1 revised: 'db-migrations overlay carries recall patterns for all five named topics (delete+replace migration symmetry — both sides, destructive migrations, backfill correctness, expand/contract sequencing, lock duration) AND FP-discrimination guards for each.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'dso content' with a concrete file path or named document (e.g., a specific section of an existing doc, or a new doc to be created) that contains the recall patterns and FP-guards to be imported into the overlay.
findings.finding: 'dso content' is named as the source for both the recall patterns and the FP-guards, but 'dso' does not identify any resolvable file, document, or artifact in the repository — an executor cannot locate the source material.
findings.location: What section: 'Enrich the db-migrations overlay prompt with dso content: recall patterns AND FP-guards'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'passing' for the eval in AC2: specify the minimum number of recall fixtures, the minimum number of no-fire fixtures, and the required pass rate (e.g. '≥5 recall fixtures all fire; ≥3 FP guard fixtures none fire').
findings.finding: AC2 ('passing recall + no-fire eval') does not define what 'passing' means: no fixture count, no pass-rate threshold, and no description of what constitutes a recall hit vs. miss. An executing agent cannot determine when this criterion is satisfied.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval; does not over-suppress valid patterns.'
findings.suggested_fix: Revise AC2 to: 'Lands with a passing eval: all N recall fixtures fire and all M no-fire fixtures are suppressed (N≥5, M≥3); no previously-passing fixture regresses.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving step for AC3, e.g. 'diff code-review-verify.md against HEAD~1 shows no db-migration-specific rule additions' or a grep command that asserts no migration keywords appear in the verifier file.
findings.finding: AC3 ('Overlay remains advisory; no domain rubric leaks into code-review-verify.md') has no described proving step or command; nothing in the plan explains how an agent would verify this constraint holds after the change.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'Overlay remains advisory; no domain rubric leaks into code-review-verify.md.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3: 'Verified by: `git diff HEAD -- src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-verify.md` shows no db-migration-domain additions.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete file path for the new eval spec (e.g., `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-db-migrations.eval.yaml`) in the Files list, matching the eval harness's discovery convention.
findings.finding: The second Files entry — 'eval spec + fixtures for the db-migrations overlay (recall + no-fire cases)' — specifies no concrete file path, and no `code-review-db-migrations.eval.yaml` (or any `code-review-*.eval.yaml`) exists anywhere in `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/`. This is a new file creation with a hallucinated/missing path.
findings.location: Files — 'eval spec + fixtures for the db-migrations overlay'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-db-migrations.eval.yaml` as the explicit file path in the Files section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale for why inline prompt enrichment (vs. alternatives like structured evidence-grounding instructions or pattern exemplars) is the right form for the db-migrations recall patterns and FP-guards named in the plan.
findings.finding: The plan states the architectural constraint motivating the approach ('locked principle that domain rubric lives in the overlay, not the generic Pass-2 verifier') but gives no positive rationale for WHY inline prompt enrichment is the right mechanism for db-migrations recall improvement — i.e., why this form of enrichment (vs. structured checklists, evidence-grounding instructions, or pattern libraries) is well-suited to the specific recall patterns named (delete+replace symmetry, destructive migrations, backfill correctness, expand/contract sequencing, lock duration).

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
